other_observers: Seen with Rachel Hopper on Dec 18 and Rachel, Joey Kellner, Andrew Spencer, Cole Wild, and Loch Kilpatrick on January 29.; Not sure.
duration: About 10 minutes the first day and 20 minutes the other
specific_location: Fort Collins City Park (Dec 18 2005) and Longs Pond (Jan 29 2006)
description: In size, the Brant was noticeably smaller (1/3rd) than adjacent Canada Geese. Overall impressions were of a very stocky, short-necked, stub-nosed, dark goose. The head, neck and upper breast were a dark black except for a white stripe/barring pattern on the throat and upper neck. Mantle and upper-wing coloration was a dark brown. The middle and lower breast was dark brown which separates the western race from the more eastern American race (hrota); hrota birds typically have a whitish breast and belly with numerous brown striations. The flanks of the subject bird did appear noticeably lighter (almost whitish) from that of the dark brown belly and breast. Remiges appeared black and the tail and upper-tail coverts were white.Behaviors: Seen both dates swimming within a large group of Canada Geese.Call: None heardPlumage: 1st cycle
